title: Trouble COPYing from symlinked directory that was mounted into podman with machine init -v
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have a symlinked directory /store/waamount that points to /Users/wnm3/csnext/aiden/ai-c-aiden/common/WAAUXServer/data
I have initialized my machine passing -v /store/waamount:/store/waamount
This has allowed me to run containers from images I'd already built, using the -v /store/waamount:<some_internal_location> without any problems.

However, when attempting to build a new image the Dockerfile gets to a point where it tries to copy content from the /store/waamount into the image and it fails to find the data to be copied. The following is the line that fails:
COPY --chown=1002:0 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json ./data/TenantInfo.json

Returns this during image build:
STEP 18/22: COPY --chown=1002:0 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json ./data/TenantInfo.json
Error: error building at STEP "COPY --chown=1002:0 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json ./data/TenantInfo.json": checking on sources under "/var/tmp/libpod_builder244888758/build": copier: stat: "/store/waamount/TenantInfo.json": no such file or directory

If I perform this command from the directory where I'm performing the build it shows the file exists:
ls -l /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json
-rwxrwxrwx  1 wnm3  staff  13488 Mar 17 12:13 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a symbolic link from a directory to data that you'd like to copy into the image

-    (on MacOS you need to have created the /store using /etc/synthetic.conf with a line like store Users/wnm3/store) based on [this article](https://derflounder.wordpress.com/2020/01/18/creating-root-level-directories-and-symbolic-links-on-macos-catalina/)
-    cd ~ (/Users/wnm3 on my machine)
-    mkdir data
-    cd data
-    touch TenantInfo.json
-    cd /store
-    ln -s /Users/wnm3/data waamount


2. Initialize podman to mount the /store/waamount directory:

-     podman machine init -v /store/waamount:/store/waamount
-     podman machine start
-     podman machine set --rootful

3. Build an image with a Dockerfile that tries to copy /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json into the image somewhere with a command like (assuming the WORKDIR has a subdirectory data in it:

-     COPY --chown=1002:0 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json ./data/TenantInfo.json

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: error building at STEP "COPY --chown=1002:0 /store/waamount/TenantInfo.json ./data/TenantInfo.json": checking on sources under "/var/tmp/libpod_builder244888758/build": copier: stat: "/store/waamount/TenantInfo.json": no such file or directory

Though the file being copied clearly exists.


**Describe the results you expected:**
No error and for the build to continue to the next step

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
I am able to reference the mounted /store/waamount when using the -v command in the docker run command to mount it into the container, and it appears to work correctly.

Perhaps this is related to the issue of /store being a mounted directory as well as waamount being a symlink?

comments.body:
If I recall correctly, the files used with the `COPY` command needs to be in the build context.

So it doesn't support absolute path, but I guess you could pass in `/store/waamount` for the dir ?

i.e. if you run `build directory`, then `COPY /foo/bar` will actually look for `directory/foo/bar`.

So any "absolute" path will still be relative to the build context, since the remote VM is "invisible".
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I gave up. I could not get podman to work with symbolically linked directories so paid for Docker Enterprise.
I am not sure what is supposed to happen here. Nor what Docker does.

The use case is I have multiple Eclipse projects that all need test data so I have one real directory of test data and create symbolic links from the other projects to that data directory. Later when I create Docker images from the projects I copy some of the test data into the image and this wasn't working with Podman. I tried loading the entire drive into the podman VM as well as everything under my home directory. Neither worked. Docker has no issue and doesn't require the additional step to explicitly mount something in its VM.

Is everything located under $HOME?
Did you try `podman machine init -v $HOME:$HOME `?
Yes, the problem was the symbolic links during copy in the Dockerfile that were not working.

If the specified build context location is a symbolic link, it is followed, but symbolic links that are inside of the build context that point to locations outside of the build context aren't followed.  (The `podman build` operation is basically tarring up the build context and handing it to the in-VM podman process, which is not that far off from how traditional `docker build` does things, and some of this behavior is a natural consequence of that.)  As @afbjorklund noted, COPY instructions interpret source locations as being relative to the build context, because any items outside of that directory weren't supplied by the client.

Without knowing how `podman build` was invoked, I don't really know what to suggest here.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I gave up so please close as this may have been something specific to my environment.
